meta.name: Crusty Sausage Rolls (for Brats and Sausages)
meta.author: SSA Canonical Catalog
meta.description: Crusty, sturdy rolls designed to hold brats and sausages: crisp crust, chewy interior, and enough structure to handle juices and toppings. Uses a simple preferment (poolish) for flavor and a steam bake for crust. Includes commercial flour or fine fresh-milled flour guidance (choose one).

equipment.label: Baking steel/stone (recommended) or heavy sheet pan

ingredients.label: Commercial flour option (use this OR fresh-milled option): bread flour
ingredients.notes: Use with poolish flour for total ~500 g flour. Bread flour gives the best chew/structure for sausage rolls.

ingredients.label: Fresh-milled flour option (use this OR commercial option): fine-milled, sifted
ingredients.notes: Fine-mill and sift coarse bran for better gluten development. Fresh-milled absorbs more; you may need +10–30 g water.

ingredients.label: Water (main dough)
ingredients.notes: Adjust as needed; final hydration target is ~68–72% depending on flour.

ingredients.label: Extra water (fresh-milled adjustment)
ingredients.notes: Add as needed if dough is stiff/dry with fresh-milled flour.

ingredients.label: Sugar (optional, slight browning boost)
ingredients.notes: About 2 tsp. Optional; improves color if your oven bakes pale.

ingredients.label: Olive oil or neutral oil (optional, slightly softer bite)
ingredients.notes: About 2 tsp. Optional; traditional crusty rolls can omit.

ingredients.label: Instant yeast (main dough)
ingredients.notes: About 1 tsp. Poolish provides much of the fermentation.

steps.title: Make poolish (8–16 hours)
steps.body: Mix poolish flour, poolish water, and a pinch of yeast until smooth. Cover and let ferment at room temperature 8–16 hours until bubbly and domed (just starting to recede).

steps.title: Choose your flour option
steps.body: Use either the commercial flour option OR the fresh-milled option (not both). Fresh-milled should be fine-milled and sifted for best roll structure.

steps.title: Mix and autolyse (optional but recommended)
steps.body: In a large bowl, combine main flour and main water (hold back ~20 g water). Mix until no dry flour remains. Cover and rest 20 minutes. This improves extensibility and crust.

steps.title: Add poolish, salt, and yeast
steps.body: Add the poolish, salt, and main yeast (and optional sugar/oil). Mix until well combined. If dough feels stiff, add the held-back water gradually. Goal: slightly tacky dough that can be shaped into rolls.

steps.title: Develop gluten
steps.body: Knead 6–10 minutes by hand (or 4–6 minutes in mixer on low) until dough is smoother and elastic. Alternatively: do 3 sets of stretch-and-folds every 20 minutes during the first hour of bulk.

steps.title: Bulk ferment
steps.body: Cover and ferment 60–90 minutes at room temperature until puffy and expanded ~50–75%. If using folds, perform them during the first hour.

steps.title: Divide and pre-shape
steps.body: Turn dough out and divide into 8 pieces (~85–95 g each) for brat/sausage rolls. Pre-shape into tight balls. Rest 15 minutes covered.

steps.title: Shape into sausage rolls
steps.body: Shape each piece into a tight oval/log about 15–18 cm (6–7 in) long, tapered slightly at ends for a classic brat roll. Place seam-side down on parchment-lined pan.

steps.title: Proof
steps.body: Cover and proof 35–60 minutes until noticeably puffy and spring back slowly when gently pressed. Do not overproof (they’ll lose oven spring).

steps.title: Preheat oven + steam setup
steps.body: Preheat to 475°F (245°C) with baking steel/stone inside. Place an empty cast iron pan/tray on a lower rack for steam (optional but recommended).

steps.title: Score
steps.body: Score each roll with a single lengthwise slash (about 1 cm / 1/2 in deep). This controls expansion and creates a classic split top.

steps.title: Bake with steam
steps.body: Slide rolls onto hot stone/steel (or bake on a preheated sheet). Add steam: carefully pour 1 cup hot water into the steam pan (stand back from steam). Bake 10 minutes at 475°F (245°C).

steps.title: Finish bake (dry heat for crust)
steps.body: Reduce to 450°F (232°C), vent steam by removing the steam pan or cracking the door briefly, and bake 8–12 minutes more until deep golden. Rolls should feel light and sound hollow when tapped.

steps.title: Cool before slicing
steps.body: Cool on a rack at least 20 minutes before slicing. Cutting too early compresses crumb.

notes: Sausage-roll logic: slightly narrower, longer rolls fit brats better and hold toppings without collapsing.
Crust logic: steam in first part of bake enables expansion and blistered crust; dry heat finishes crispness.
Fresh-milled flour: sift coarse bran and be prepared to add a small amount of extra water; bulk may run slightly faster or slower depending on flour activity—watch the dough, not the clock.
Freezer logic: freeze fully cooled rolls. Re-crisp from frozen at 350°F (175°C) for 10–15 minutes.

storage.roomTemp: Best day-of. Store in paper bag or loosely covered up to 1 day (crust softens).
storage.freezer: Freeze airtight up to 2 months. Reheat to restore crust.
